title: boronia pilosa var. lancifolia
intro: Boronia pilosa var. lancifolia is a variant of the Boronia pilosa plant, a species of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Boronia pilosa var. lancifolia is believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
